meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Master Testing for Student Projects
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no testing knowledge and professional SaaS pricing. Learn to build resilient, revenue-ready apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a QA degree to ship. By integrating automated testing directly into your pricing logic, you ensure revenue calculations are bulletproof. Use Antigravity to scaffold your test suite and Claude Code to iterate on edge cases, transforming your student project into a production-grade, verifiable SaaS application.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect feature set, but the shadow of 'untested code' looms over your deployment. Every time you touch your pricing logic, the fear of breaking a subscription model paralyzes your progress. It’s the classic student trap: building great features but lacking the engineering discipline to prove they work under pressure.

But here is the secret: testing isn't about perfection; it’s about confidence. By treating your pricing engine as the heartbeat of your SaaS, you can learn to write tests that protect your revenue and your reputation. Today, we turn that anxiety into an asset, using AI-driven workflows to automate the verification of your most critical business logic.

steps.title: Define Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust data model for your subscription tiers and discount logic before writing a single test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including monthly/annual tiers, trial periods, and coupon logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context ./src/types/pricing.ts --instruction 'Generate unit tests for this schema to ensure boundary conditions are met.'

steps.title: Bootstrap Test Environment
steps.description: Configure your test runner to catch errors in your pricing calculations before they reach the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Vitest configuration for a Node.js project focusing on high-coverage unit tests for utility functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup vitest and configure coverage thresholds at 80%'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Write the core business logic, keeping it decoupled from the database for easier testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pure function that calculates the final price based on tier, discount code, and tax rate, ensuring it handles edge cases like zero-cost trials.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/services/pricing.ts --apply-logic 'ensure all math uses integer precision to avoid floating point errors'

steps.title: Automate Edge Case Testing
steps.description: Use AI to generate scenarios you haven't considered, like rapid-fire subscription upgrades or expired coupons.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of test cases for a pricing engine that includes invalid coupons, expired trials, and currency conversion errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate test cases for all edge scenarios in pricing logic'

steps.title: Integrate CI/CD Checks
steps.description: Ensure your tests run automatically every time you push code, preventing regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs vitest and blocks deployment if coverage falls below 80%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file .github/workflows/test.yml --instruction 'ensure workflow runs on every push to main'

pro_tips: Always use integer math (cents) for pricing calculations to avoid IEEE 754 floating point errors.
Mock your payment gateway API responses; never hit the live Stripe sandbox during local unit testing.
Prioritize testing the 'happy path' last; your first tests should always focus on how the system fails.
Use property-based testing to feed random inputs into your pricing engine to find hidden bugs.
Treat your test suite as documentation; if a test is hard to read, your pricing logic is likely too complex.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing tests are passing, move on to implementing Stripe Webhooks and verify their security using similar automated test patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the fear of bugs hold your code back. Master the art of automated testing and build a SaaS that can scale to thousands of users with total confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
